prompt: I want to trace my DSPy pipeline locally so I can see what prompts are being sent and how many tokens each call uses.
expected_output: Sets up Phoenix with local mode and auto-instrumentation for DSPy tracing
assertions: Installs arize-phoenix AND openinference-instrumentation-dspy AND openinference-instrumentation-litellm
Calls px.launch_app() to start the local UI
Uses register(auto_instrument=True) from phoenix.otel (not the legacy DSPyInstrumentor pattern)
Shows how to configure a DSPy LM and run a traced call
Mentions the UI is available at localhost:6006

prompt: My team wants to use Phoenix in the cloud so everyone can see DSPy traces. How do I set that up?
expected_output: Configures Phoenix for cloud mode with Arize platform
assertions: Sets PHOENIX_COLLECTOR_ENDPOINT to the Arize cloud URL
Does not call px.launch_app() for cloud mode (or explains it is not needed)

prompt: I have traces in Phoenix and want to automatically score whether my DSPy outputs are helpful. How do I use Phoenix evals?
expected_output: Uses Phoenix evals module to classify traced outputs
assertions: Imports llm_classify and OpenAIModel from phoenix.evals
Gets spans as a DataFrame using px.Client().get_spans_dataframe()
Defines a template with column placeholders for evaluation
Uses rails parameter to constrain classification labels
